page: Amazon Web Services g5g.4xlarge is an ARM64-based virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor architecture running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For graphics and accelerated workloads, the server integrates a single NVIDIA T4G GPU from the Turing family, providing 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not included on this instance type, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24286.82 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8557.0. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 948.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.17 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show high read speeds for small cached blocks at 946.81 GB/sec, though large uncached block writes drop to 66.22 GB/sec. The integrated NVIDIA T4G GPU delivers average performance in ray tracing and image processing. Large language model inference speeds are modest, achieving 10.67 tokens/sec for text generation on a medium 7B model, while prompt processing on the same model is limited to 16.13 tokens/sec.

The g5g.4xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-based GPU acceleration, combining custom AWS silicon with NVIDIA's Turing architecture. This resource density makes it suitable for cost-sensitive graphics-intensive applications, Android game streaming, and lightweight machine learning inference. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and the lower single-core CPU performance represent notable tradeoffs for disk-heavy or highly sequential workloads. It is best utilized for containerized microservices, static web serving, software compilation, and entry-level GPU-accelerated workloads that do not require high-speed local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.4xlarge is an ARM64-based virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AWS Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. Designed for graphics-intensive and accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, though it lacks local storage. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and GPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24286.82 and a Geekbench ray tracing score of 13953.0, alongside moderate LLM inference capabilities. This server offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for ARM-native GPU applications, making it suitable for Android game streaming, lightweight machine learning inference, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.4xlarge is an ARM64 server with 16 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM, optimized for graphics-intensive ARM workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.4xlarge features 16 ARM64 cores, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for cost-efficient graphics and machine learning workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based accelerated instance featuring dedicated Neoverse cores and an NVIDIA GPU for cost-efficient graphics and lightweight machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Features a single NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM
Lacks local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Best suited for ARM-native graphics, Android game streaming, and light ML inference
